data_sources.source_name: ABC Corp Shipping Transactions Dec 2025 – May 2026

proxy_scope.target_company_context: ABC Food Logistics Co., Ltd. — Fresh seafood & meat distributor
proxy_scope.disclaimer: This analysis is automatically generated by LogiHub Engine v1.0 running on ABC Corp enterprise sample data. The analytical framework and optimization logic are of production-grade quality. All absolute figures are for demonstration purposes only.

product_families.description: Fresh and frozen seafood, fresh meat, processed meat products requiring cold chain

overloaded_hubs.reason: Seollal peak (Jan 2026) drives 1,896 tons inbound — 41% above cold storage capacity of 800 tons. Spoilage risk: HIGH.

underused_hubs.reason: Healthy utilization but will reach watchlist during simultaneous Seollal + spring peaks.

high_cost_lanes.recommended_action: Reroute via Gyeonggi hub for final delivery
Add Gwangju hub as intermediate consolidation point
Consolidate with Daegu pickup to improve load factor

diagnosis.summary: ABC Corp's current 2-hub network is critically strained. The Busan cold storage hub operates at 112.5% peak utilization during Seollal, risking product spoilage and SLA failures across 4 regions. Southern Korea (Jeonbuk, Jeonnam, Gwangju) has SLA violation rates exceeding 30%. Immediate network expansion is required.

scenarios.description: Current 2-hub network: Busan + Gyeonggi. Severely overloaded at Busan during Seollal peak.
scenarios.managerial_interpretation: Status quo is unsustainable. Busan hub exceeds cold storage capacity in January and April-May, causing SLA failures across southern Korea.

scenarios.description: 3-hub config adding Incheon. Relieves import seafood pressure and covers Seoul metro better.
scenarios.managerial_interpretation: Incheon hub absorbs imported seafood flows efficiently. Reduces long-haul Busan-Seoul lanes by ~28%. However, southern Korea (Gwangju, Jeonnam) remains underserved.

scenarios.description: 3-hub config adding Gwangju. Fixes southern coverage gap for Jeonnam/Jeonbuk supply lanes.
scenarios.managerial_interpretation: Gwangju hub dramatically improves SLA for the southern supply corridor. Fresh seafood from Jeonnam reaches consumers 3.2 hours faster. Cost slightly higher than S1 due to lower Gwangju hub utilization.

scenarios.description: Recommended. Busan + Gyeonggi + Incheon + Gwangju. Optimal cost-service balance for cold chain.
scenarios.managerial_interpretation: S3 achieves 91.8% SLA compliance — up from 72.4% baseline — while reducing peak utilization from 112.5% to 74.1% at Busan. Annual savings of $87K vs. baseline despite additional fixed costs, driven by shorter transport lanes and reduced spoilage losses.

scenarios.description: 5-hub config adds Daejeon central node. Near-perfect SLA but highest fixed cost.
scenarios.managerial_interpretation: S4 achieves best-in-class SLA at 96.3% but costs $32K/year more than S3 with marginal improvement. Daejeon hub is underutilized at ~45%. Recommended only if the enterprise targets premium express delivery positioning.

recommended_network.recommendation_summary: Expand from 2 to 4 cold-chain hubs to eliminate Busan overflow and fix southern-Korea SLA gaps.

recommended_hubs.reason_selected: Primary seafood supply hub. Must be retained and capacity-managed.
Central distribution hub for the Seoul metro corridor — highest demand density.
Captures imported seafood at port-of-entry, eliminating long-haul cold chain from Busan for imports.
Fixes the critical southern-Korea coverage gap. 3 regions currently at >30% SLA violation.

recommended_network.why_this_scenario: S3 is the only scenario that simultaneously resolves Busan overflow (Critical risk), closes the southern-Korea coverage gap (High risk), and achieves positive ROI within 9 months.
why_not_other_scenarios: S0 (baseline): Busan overload is unsustainable — Critical spoilage risk each Jan and May peak.
S1 (Incheon only): Fixes import flows but leaves Jeonnam/Jeonbuk at >40% SLA violation.
S2 (Gwangju only): Fixes southern gap but Incheon import flows remain inefficient.
S4 (5-hub): Marginal SLA gain of +4.5% over S3 at $32K/yr additional cost — B/C ratio < 1.5.

seasonal_playbook.risk: Busan hub overflow (112.5%) — fresh seafood spoilage risk during Seollal (Jan 28-30 2026). Peak demand 1,896 tons vs. 800-ton cold storage capacity.
recommended_actions: Pre-position 35% of Fresh_Food inventory at Gyeonggi hub 10 days before Seollal
Activate temporary cold-storage 3PL at Busan (Shinsegae Logistics, +400 tons) from Jan 15
Freeze Busan hub inbound at 85% capacity; reroute overflow to Gyeonggi
Deploy 6 additional refrigerated trucks on Busan-Seoul corridor from Jan 20-Feb 5
seasonal_playbook.de_escalation_rule: When Busan utilization drops below 70% for 5 consecutive days post-Seollal, terminate 3PL contract and release temporary trucks.

seasonal_playbook.risk: Parents' Day (May 8) and Children's Day (May 5) drive 18% demand surge for premium seafood and meat gift sets. Gyeonggi hub approaches 95% utilization.
recommended_actions: Pre-stock gift-set SKUs (premium seafood, wagyu cuts) at Gyeonggi hub 2 weeks ahead
Partner with Coupang Fulfillment for e-commerce overflow in Seoul/Incheon
Add Saturday operations (normally off) for May 3-10 at both hubs
seasonal_playbook.de_escalation_rule: Resume standard Mon-Fri operations from May 12. Review gift-set inventory levels; mark down slow SKUs after May 15.

business_case.risk_reduction_summary: Eliminates Busan cold-storage overflow risk (Critical→Low). Reduces southern-Korea SLA violations from avg 38.6% to under 8%. Fresh-food spoilage rate projected to drop from 3.2% to 0.9%.
business_case.executive_summary: ABC Corp's 2-hub network loses an estimated $146,500/year in spoilage costs and SLA penalties. Expanding to a 4-hub cold chain (S3: Busan + Gyeonggi + Incheon + Gwangju) generates $106,122 in net annual savings — a payback period of 11.6 months. SLA compliance improves from 72.4% to 91.8%, directly reducing customer churn risk. Recommendation: Execute Immediately (B/C ratio: 2.03).

actions: Sign emergency 3PL cold-storage contract at Busan (+400 tons)
Retrain dispatch team on overflow routing protocols
roadmap.expected_impact: Eliminate Critical overload risk before next peak. Busan utilization capped at 85%.

actions: Sign 3-year lease at Incheon Port cold facility (CAND_ICN)
Install blast-freezer units for imported seafood (budget: $28K)
Migrate import-seafood flows (Incheon→Seoul, Incheon→Gyeonggi) to new hub
Integrate WMS with existing Gyeonggi hub system
roadmap.expected_impact: Import seafood SLA from 71% to 94%. Gyeonggi hub load reduced by 22%.

actions: Sign lease at Gwangju Fresh Hub (CAND_GJU)
Onboard 2 regional sales teams for Jeonbuk/Jeonnam markets
Pilot cross-docking with Jeonbuk meat suppliers
roadmap.expected_impact: Southern-Korea SLA violation drops from 38% to <8%. New market revenue: est. $95K/year.

actions: Run quarterly LogiHub re-analysis with updated transaction data
Implement dynamic routing between all 4 hubs based on real-time utilization
Negotiate volume discounts with refrigerated trucking partners (target -8% rate)
Prepare for Chuseok 2027 peak with full 4-hub capacity playbook
roadmap.expected_impact: Achieve 91.8%+ SLA sustained. Full $savings realized. Network stress-test resilience: 94/100.
